findings.title: Misleading forbidden message when no session org
findings.description: When getCurrentOrgId() returns null, orgId is undefined and the guard `!orgId` fires — but the error says "Task does not belong to your organization" even though the real reason is no session org. The parallel guard in code-review.ts (requireOrgMatch) correctly says "org_id must match session org (none)". The ambiguous message makes debugging harder and breaks the invariant that all null-session-org errors surface a consistent diagnostic.

findings.title: Cross-org task ID enumeration via distinct errors
findings.description: The guard returns 'forbidden' with message 'Task does not belong to your organization' when the task exists but belongs to another org, versus 'not_found' when the task doesn't exist. This distinction allows an attacker to enumerate valid task IDs across org boundaries — they can confirm a task ID is valid by observing the forbidden vs not_found response. A fail-closed approach should return the same 'not_found' error for both cases.

findings.title: Error message leaks session org ID to caller
findings.description: requireOrgMatch returns `org_id must match session org (${sessionOrg || 'none'})`, revealing the authenticated session's org ID (or the fact that no session org exists) to the tool caller. While org IDs are low-sensitivity, consolidating this message into a shared function used by three handlers increases the exposure surface. A generic message would prevent enumeration of session state.

findings.title: Skill access by ID bypasses org-scoping (IDOR)
findings.description: The load_skill (line 191) and list_skill_files (line 261) cases fetch skills via skillsManager.getSkill(skillId), which reads directly from Firestore with no org filter. Meanwhile listAvailableSkills() (line 128) correctly scopes by orgId — confirming skills are org-scoped resources. Any authenticated user can read org-scoped skill content, instructions, and files from another org by supplying the ID. The PR adds an org check for the recommend_skills→task path but misses the two skill-by-ID paths in the same handler.
